title: Jonesiella arcta
intro: Jonesiella arcta, also known as the narrow-leaved Jonesiella, is a species of plant in the family Apiaceae, native to Australia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various skin conditions and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Jonesiella arcta include volatile oils, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infused oils, which are commonly used to treat skin irritations and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Jonesiella arcta is a species of plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Jonesiella, which is part of the tribe Gnaphalieae. The tribe Gnaphalieae is further classified under the subfamily Gnaphaloideae. This subfamily is a part of the family Asteraceae. The classification of Jonesiella arcta is still a subject of ongoing research and may be subject to change.
